title: hieracium phlomoides subsp. bowlesianum
intro: Hieracium phlomoides subsp. bowlesianum, also known as Bowles' hawkweed, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin irritations. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Hieracium phlomoides subsp. bowlesianum can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
